tasks.title: SubFlow Phase 1: Bid Request Management and Document Generation
tasks.description: Build the Phase 1 foundation of SubFlow. Web based, fast loading, login required. Core features: manage incoming bid requests, generate RFQ (Request for Quote), RFI (Request for Information), and full proposals. Proposal must include project name, address, date, revision, customer info, cover letter, price, drawings used, detailed description, clarifications, work not included, price breakdown, terms, alternates (optional), initials on every page, signature on one page.

tasks.title: SubFlow Phase 2: Email Delivery
tasks.description: Add ability to send proposals, RFI, RFQ, and decline to bid directly to customers via email from within SubFlow.

tasks.title: SubFlow Phase 4: Email Forwarding for New Bids and Full Takeoff Software
tasks.description: Create new bidding opportunities by forwarding an email into SubFlow. Also include a full takeoff software module.

tasks.title: SubFlow Phase 5: Full Estimating Software
tasks.description: Build out a full estimating software module inside SubFlow.

tasks.title: SubFlow Phase 6+: Full Project Management
tasks.description: Expand SubFlow into a full project management platform for subcontractors.

tasks.title: Build Reverse-Engineering Estimating Calculator (React Component)
tasks.description: Create a single React component that reverse-engineers labor hours and material costs from a target price. Features: labor/material split slider, labor and material overhead/profit tracking, material adjustment and tax basis options, real-time calculation, and localStorage-based settings persistence. Calculator should output detailed breakdown for labor and material with live updates. Include collapsible Settings panel with Save/Reset buttons.

tasks.title: Redesign BackCalc for Better UX and Visual Appeal
tasks.description: Review the current BackCalc reverse-engineering estimating calculator and propose a more pleasing and user-friendly design. The redesign should: (1) Improve visual hierarchy and overall aesthetics while maintaining the purple gradient theme or suggesting a better one, (2) Enhance user experience and practical usability of the input and results layout, (3) NOT impact loading or runtime performance — all changes must be CSS/design only, no functionality changes, (4) Keep the tool fast and responsive. Provide design mockups or code suggestions for the visual improvements. Focus on making the calculator more intuitive and visually appealing while keeping it practical to use.

tasks.title: Build Electrical Estimating Tools Hub Website
tasks.description:
Design and build a single-page website that acts as a hub for electrical estimating tools. The website should be visually impressive, clean, modern, and fun with a professional but approachable vibe.

Core Requirements:
- Single-page website with clean, modern, and fun design
- All navigation links displayed as attractive buttons (not plain text links)
- Responsive and user-friendly layout
- Professional yet approachable aesthetic

Links to Include:
1. WireCalc: https://wirecalc.vercel.app/
2. V0 Site Calc: https://v0-site-calc.vercel.app/
3. BackCalc: https://sbexpress.github.io/backcalc/
4. PKA Dashboard: https://pka-git-master-birnbaumshaya-gmailcoms-projects.vercel.app/dashboard

Design Preferences:
- Clean and organized layout
- Professional but approachable vibe
- Prominent, easy-to-navigate buttons
- Creative with overall aesthetic and color scheme
- Maintain focus on functionality and user experience

Feel free to be creative with the design while keeping it functional and user-friendly!

✅ COMPLETED: iOS-style static HTML app with gray/silver color palette. Ready for publishing.
